pr.title: it: Match it_266F, itsonans, and itbox functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched several item-system functions in it_266F, itsonans, and itbox, recorded three new 100% scratch matches, partially improved two more functions, and cleaned up noisy exploratory code in it_2E5A. The strongest evidence is the PR body and diff: it_8026E7E0, itSonans_UnkMotion2_Coll, and it_3F14_Logic1_Thrown were marked 100% matched; it_8026E32C and itSonans_UnkMotion2_Phys were described as partial. The changes mainly refine collision/physics handling, item thrown-state behavior, stack padding, and typed header declarations.
what_changed: Added scratch entries marking it_8026E7E0, itSonans_UnkMotion2_Coll, and it_3F14_Logic1_Thrown as 100% MATCHED.

Reworked it_8026E7E0 in src/melee/it/it_266F.c to explicitly load Item and CollData, call it_80276214, use mpColl_800471F8, copy coll->cur_pos into item->pos, update item->xC30 from coll->floor.index when collision is detected, OR in it_80276308 and it_802763E0 results, and use a local chk_copy when calling it_80276D9C.

Adjusted stack padding in nearby it_266F functions, replacing PAD_STACK(14) with a u8 _[22] local in it_8026E15C, changing PAD_STACK(18) to PAD_STACK(26) in it_8026E248, adding PAD_STACK(42) to the disabled it_8026E32C attempt, and increasing it_8026F6BC padding from 80 to 96.

Expanded the disabled/partial it_8026E32C implementation attempt with explicit collision setup and status flag handling similar to it_8026E7E0; PR body says this function was only partially matched at 95.3%.

Cleaned up it_802E61C4 in it_2E5A.c by removing many exploratory comments and unused locals, using attr = item->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, computing var_r29 through a float-pointer view of attr[1], and preserving the minimum return value of 1.
Changed it_802E6A74 in it_2E5A.c to cast the address of item->xDD4_itemVar.it_2E5A.x1C to it_2E5A_ItemVars_struct* so the inline struct at offset 0x1C can be accessed for x8/x14 and lb_8000B1CC.
Updated it_3F14_Logic1_Thrown in itbox.c to call it_8026B3A8 before Item_80268E5C and to pass ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE | ITEM_DROP_UPDATE instead of only ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.
Typed it_802EAAEC in itleadead.h as void it_802EAAEC(Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, int, float) and added ft/forward.h for Fighter_GObj.
Implemented itSonans_UnkMotion2_Phys with item physics/setup calls gated by it_8027A09C, then assigned item hitlag callbacks to efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll; PR body says this was only partial.
Matched itSonans_UnkMotion2_Coll by returning it_8027A118(gobj, (HSD_GObjEvent) it_802CD4D8) instead of false.
smart_moves: Used explicit Item* and CollData* locals in collision functions to control load order and make the decompiled flow match the original more closely.
Copied the collision result into a separate s32 chk_copy before calling it_80276D9C, a small ordering/type tactic that likely helped it_8026E7E0 reach 100%.
Preserved stack layout with targeted padding changes, including replacing a PAD_STACK macro with a concrete u8 local array where needed.
Cleaned it_2E5A.c by removing large blocks of failed exploratory code once a simpler matching expression was found, improving future readability.
Used a cast to the address of an inline item-var field in it_802E6A74, documenting that the goal was to access an embedded struct at offset 0x1C rather than follow a pointer.
Moved it_802EAAEC from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to a typed declaration and added the needed forward declaration include, reducing header ambiguity for future matching.
Recorded matched functions in scratches.txt immediately with author and match status, keeping project matching metadata synchronized with code changes.
decomp_lessons: For item collision callbacks, matching may require manually spelling out the common sequence: get Item, take &item->x378_itemColl, call it_80276214, call mpColl_800471F8, copy coll->cur_pos back to item->pos, conditionally update floor index, then OR in additional collision/status checks.
Small local-variable choices can matter: a separate copy of a collision bitfield, explicit bool/s32 usage, or a named CollData* may affect codegen enough to match.
Stack padding should be tuned per function and does not always need to stay as PAD_STACK; a concrete u8 array can be the right representation when the compiler layout demands it.
When an item var field is actually an inline struct stored at an offset, casting the address of the field can be more accurate than treating the field as a pointer.
Array/attribute layout uncertainty can sometimes be resolved with a float-pointer view, as in ((f32*) &attr[1])[arg2], but this should be kept evidence-grounded and localized.

Item state functions that appeared empty may need calls that initialize animation/drop behavior and hitlag callbacks; in itsonans, efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll were assigned through Item fields.
Header typing work can be part of matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, int, and float parameters required adding ft/forward.h.
naming_conventions: Matched item functions retained existing project names such as it_8026E7E0, itSonans_UnkMotion2_Coll, and it_3F14_Logic1_Thrown.
Project item object aliases used in typed declarations and locals include Item_GObj, Fighter_GObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_GObjEvent, Item, and CollData.
Item update flags are named ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and ITEM_DROP_UPDATE and can be ORed for Item_80268E5C.
Collision/item fields in this slice use established x-offset names such as x378_itemColl, xC30, xD50_landNum, xC4_article_data, x4_specialAttributes, and xDD4_itemVar.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Tune stack padding values directly to match compiler stack layout: examples include PAD_STACK(26), PAD_STACK(42), PAD_STACK(96), and u8 pad[40].
Use explicit collision-bitfield accumulation with `chk = chk | it_80276308(...)` and `chk = chk | it_802763E0(...)` rather than relying only on compound operators when ordering/codegen matters.
Introduce temporary locals such as `s32 chk_copy = chk` to influence register allocation or argument passing while preserving semantics.
Use pointer casts for data-layout matching, including `(it_2E5A_ItemVars_struct*) &item->xDD4_itemVar.it_2E5A.x1C` and `((f32*) &attr[1])[arg2]`.
Prefer concrete typed prototypes over UNK declarations when enough evidence exists; this can unblock cleaner callsites and include dependencies.
For simple collision wrappers, a matched function may reduce to returning a known helper call with a casted callback: `it_8027A118(gobj, (HSD_GObjEvent) it_802CD4D8)`.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly reported three 100% matches and two partial matches; there is no evidence in the slice of reviewer-requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching it_8026E32C to see how the 95.3% partial match was completed.
Inspect later PRs touching itSonans_UnkMotion2_Phys to see what remained unmatched after this partial implementation.
Search for reuse of the it_8026E7E0 collision sequence in other item collision callbacks.
Search for further cleanup or typing around it_802EAAEC and itleadead.h after this header change.
Check whether the casted inline-struct access in it_802E6A74 informed later item-var struct definitions.
